The Adler Archive includes letters, documents, manuscripts, images, magazine and newspaper articles, books, sound and video recordings, and other ephemera documenting the history of hip hop. BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Bill Adler is an American music journalist and critic who specializes in hip hop. He was director of publicity at Def Jam Recordings between 1984 and 1990. Since the early 1980s he has promoted hip hop in a variety of capacities, including as publicist, biographer, record label executive, museum consultant, curator, and documentary filmmaker.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
The Adler Archive includes letters, documents, manuscripts, images, magazine and newspaper articles, books, sound and video recordings, and other ephemera documenting the history of hip hop. At the core of the Archive are files on more than 1400 hip hop artists and groups from the 1970s to the present. The files include press clippings, promotional head shots, record label press releases, promotional flyers and other ephemera.
The Adler Collection formed the basis for much of the illustrated content in the popular book Def Jam recordings: The first 25 years of the last great record label (2011).
SERIES LIST
Series I. News Files Boxes 1-28; Box 45
Box 45 is RESTRICTED until 2050
News Files 1980s Performer Names A-H Performer Names I-P Performer Names Q-Z
News Files 1990s and 2000s Performer Names A-H Performer Names I-P Performer Names Q-Z
Series II. Posters and Framed Items Box 29, Mapcases
Series III. Records and Other Media Boxes 30-44
Separated Material Includes books catalogued individually in the Division of Rare and Manuscript collections.

Adler Hip Hop Archive, #8092. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Access Restrictions:
Access to Box 45 restricted until 2050.
Restrictions on Use:
Researchers may view one folder at a time.
Conditions Governing Use
Due to the fragility and potential degradation of moving image and sound recordings, viewing and listening is limited to items that have been digitized. If an item is in another media format, you may request to have the item digitized for access. Information on ordering access copies may be found on the Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections webpage.
Conditions Governing Use
Use of digitized audio visual materials is limited to on-site access only.
Conditions Governing Access
Selected materials have been removed from boxes 1-28 and are unavailable until digitization project is complete. Materials remaining in the boxes are available for research.
